candidates.name: Acculturation
candidates.summary: Build shared cultural understanding of why AI matters before anyone touches a tool. Addresses fear, misconceptions, and resistance at the organisational level so that skills training later sticks.
mechanics: Run facilitated sessions (e.g. 'Cafés IA') where staff debate AI impacts in their own words before any product demo.
Leadership communicates a clear narrative: what the company believes about AI and why it matters.
Surface personal mental models and misconceptions; correct them with evidence, not hype.
Establish shared vocabulary (what AI can and cannot do) before any hands-on practice.
Follow acculturation immediately with skills training — the two must be adjacent, not separated.
proof: Learner can articulate three specific ways AI affects their job or industry.
Team agrees on a shared definition of AI and its limits.
Self-reported anxiety about AI drops; curiosity and willingness to experiment rise.

known_uses.name: Cafés IA (citizen debate sessions)
known_uses.org: Centre Inffo / French public training sector
known_uses.quote: la formation n'est efficace que si elle est précédée par la diffusion d'une culture générale
known_uses.note: Gilles Babinet, co-président du Conseil national du numérique, cited in 2024 Centre Inffo analysis of French AI training effectiveness.

known_uses.name: Acculturation IA Générative offering
known_uses.org: Multiple French training providers (Suricats Consulting, SavoirIA, Algos AI)
known_uses.quote: L'acculturation de l'IA est un processus de transformation culturelle et organisationnelle visant à intégrer l'intelligence artificielle dans l'ADN de l'entreprise
known_uses.note: Distinct market category in France by 2024, offered by multiple specialist providers.

known_uses.name: Tech Show Paris 2025: Formation & IA: Acculturation pour une révolution digitale réussie
known_uses.quote: Formation & IA : Acculturation pour une révolution digitale réussie
known_uses.note: Dedicated conference session in 2024 treating acculturation as a prerequisite to digital-revolution success.

sources.title: L'acculturation, facteur clé d'efficacité des formations à l'IA
sources.quote: créer les conditions d'une appropriation collective de l'IA et de ses enjeux

sources.title: Acculturation à l'IA : le guide pour l'entreprise
sources.quote: L'acculturation de l'IA est un processus de transformation culturelle et organisationnelle

candidates.name: Whole-Crew Baseline
candidates.summary: Roll out a common AI literacy baseline to every person in an organisation — not just technical staff. Everyone learns the same foundational vocabulary, responsible-use rules, and at least one AI tool, so the whole crew can participate in AI change.
mechanics: Define a minimum literacy bar: what every employee must be able to do and know (e.g. use a chat AI, spot hallucinations, report a misuse).
Deliver via a short self-paced module (typically 3–8 hours) with role-specific worked examples.
Gate tool access or unlock further training tracks on successful completion of the baseline.
Pair with a responsible-use policy so the literacy module and the rules land together.
Track completion company-wide; use completion rate as the org-health metric, not scores.
proof: Verifiable completion rate across all departments (target: 80%+ within six months).
Every graduate can demonstrate at least one AI-assisted work task.
Responsible-use acknowledgement signed alongside module completion.

known_uses.name: IKEA AI literacy rollout
known_uses.quote: The programme offers a range of learning materials for understanding and using AI
known_uses.note: 40,000 employees trained through August 2024 (out of ~165,000); includes responsible AI and ethics training.

known_uses.name: JPMorgan Chase prompt engineering baseline
known_uses.quote: This year, everyone coming in here will have prompt engineering training to get them ready for the AI of the future
known_uses.note: CEO mandate: all incoming staff receive prompt engineering as a baseline literacy move.

known_uses.name: MasterCard responsible-AI hub
known_uses.quote: Eight hours of video training on responsible AI principles (fairness, transparency)
known_uses.note: Delivered via a new company intranet hub, launched August 2024.

known_uses.name: Elements of AI — EU 1% citizen goal
known_uses.quote: train one per cent of EU citizens in the basics of artificial intelligence
known_uses.note: Over 2 million enrolled across 170+ countries in 40+ languages; the canonical national-scale version of this move.

sources.title: These companies are training all their staff on AI, here's why
sources.quote: Large companies such as MasterCard, JPMorgan Chase and S&P Global are rolling out programmes to help prepare employees across the organisation for the AI era, not just technical staff

sources.title: Elements of AI — A free online introduction to artificial intelligence for non-experts
sources.quote: AI is going to have as big an impact on our society as electricity

sources.title: EU AI Act Article 4: AI literacy obligation
sources.quote: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall take measures to ensure, to their best extent, a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems on their behalf

candidates.name: AI-as-Mentor
candidates.summary: The AI tool itself coaches the learner: it asks Socratic questions, gives context-sensitive feedback, and never just hands over the answer. Learning and doing happen in the same environment, removing the gap between training and work.
mechanics: Learner poses a real work problem to the AI in the same tool they will use day-to-day.
AI is configured (via system prompt or course scaffolding) to guide rather than solve: it asks questions, offers hints, and reflects errors back.
Feedback is immediate and in-context; no separate lesson environment to context-switch into.
Periodically the learner reflects (in writing or aloud) on what they learned about prompting.
Completion is measured by solved problems, not time-on-task.
proof: Learner solves a real task end-to-end using only AI assistance (no human tutor).
Learner can explain which prompting choices led to better outputs.

known_uses.name: OpenAI AI Foundations — ChatGPT as tutor and practice space
known_uses.quote: A full learning experience is available directly inside ChatGPT, where learners can practice real tasks, receive feedback in context, and reflect on their work in a single environment
known_uses.note: AI Foundations course launched 2025/2026; ChatGPT acts as tutor, practice space, and feedback loop simultaneously.

known_uses.name: Khanmigo (Khan Academy AI tutor)
known_uses.quote: Unlike other AI tools such as ChatGPT, Khanmigo doesn't just give answers. Instead, with limitless patience, it guides learners to find the answer themselves.
known_uses.note: Piloted in 260+ US school districts; available free to teachers in 40+ countries via Microsoft partnership.

known_uses.name: Anthropic AI Fluency course — Claude as learning partner
known_uses.quote: This course has something for everyone, whether you're new to Claude or a seasoned AI practitioner.
known_uses.note: Free structured course; learners use Claude while taking the course about Claude — AI-as-mentor pattern embedded by design.

sources.title: Launching our first OpenAI Certifications courses
sources.quote: ChatGPT acts as the tutor, the practice space, and the feedback loop

sources.title: Khanmigo for learners: Always-available tutor, powered by AI
sources.quote: it guides learners to find the answer themselves

sources.title: AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations — Anthropic
sources.quote: I want to prepare people for the long term. Not just how to use our latest features, but how to think about and work with AI

candidates.name: Home-Forged Training
candidates.summary: Training is built by your own subject-matter experts, not bought off the shelf. Because the examples, language, and tools match the learner's actual job, the content earns more trust and sees higher completion and retention.
mechanics: Identify internal champions who already use AI well; they author or co-author the course material.
Use real company workflows, real data shapes, and real tool configurations as worked examples.
Gate the course behind an internal qualification; treat passing as a professional credential inside the org.
Publish completion rates and pass rates publicly within the company to create social proof.
Iterate the course quarterly as tools and policies change — internal authors can move faster than vendors.
proof: High completion rate (e.g. 90%+ of enrolled staff) because content is seen as directly relevant.
Graduates demonstrate the skill on internal tasks, not synthetic exercises.

known_uses.name: CyberAgent ChatGPT Enterprise 101 and custom internal workshop series
known_uses.quote: more than 100 employees participating in each of over ten sessions that ranged from beginner-friendly introductions such as 'ChatGPT Enterprise 101' to workshops on custom GPTs, hands-on Codex sessions, and internal hackathons
known_uses.note: ChatGPT Enterprise reached 93% monthly active user rate. Sessions were hosted by OpenAI but delivered as internal programming; the home-forged pattern is visible in the internally-customised content tracks.

known_uses.name: Anthropic AI Fluency CC-licensed framework for internal adaptation
known_uses.quote: HR and L&D leaders are implementing this framework by… adapting the Creative Commons–licensed content for internal programs
known_uses.note: Anthropic explicitly licenses the 4D framework for organisations to build their own home-forged courses on top of it.

sources.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
sources.quote: ChatGPT Enterprise reached a monthly active user rate of 93% at CyberAgent

sources.title: Anthropic's AI Fluency Course: How to Upskill Your Org with the 4D Framework
sources.quote: adapting the Creative Commons–licensed content for internal programs

candidates.name: Regulatory Literacy Mandate
candidates.summary: A legal or regulatory obligation forces organisations to establish a minimum AI literacy baseline for all staff who work with AI systems. The mandate turns literacy from optional to mandatory and creates documentation pressure that drives real training investment.
mechanics: Map every staff role that touches an AI system to identify who is in scope.
Design role-specific literacy tracks, not a single generic course (regulatory guidance flags generic training as insufficient).
Document the training, the completion records, and the review cycle — the obligation is best-efforts, but evidence is required.
Include coverage of the specific risks of the AI systems in scope, not only generic AI literacy.
Review and update training annually or when systems or use-cases change.
proof: Documented training records covering all in-scope staff.
Role-specific training content that references the actual AI tools and use-cases the learner encounters.

known_uses.name: EU AI Act Article 4 — AI literacy obligation
known_uses.quote: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall take measures to ensure, to their best extent, a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems on their behalf
known_uses.note: Entered into application 2 February 2025. No grace period. Applies to any European employer whose staff uses AI tools professionally.

known_uses.name: AI Literacy Programs in Europe — supporting Article 4
known_uses.quote: Generic or non-role-specific training has been identified in regulatory guidance as insufficient to meet the legal standard.
known_uses.note: Catalogues EU-recognised literacy programs that satisfy the Article 4 standard.

sources.title: Article 4: AI literacy — EU Artificial Intelligence Act
sources.quote: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall take measures to ensure, to their best extent, a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff

sources.title: EU AI Act Article 4: AI Literacy Obligation for Providers and Deployers (2026)
sources.quote: The obligation applies now. The European Commission has confirmed there is no grace period

sources.title: AI literacy is now a legal duty: Article 4 of the EU AI Act, in plain language
sources.quote: A deployer is anyone using an AI system in a professional capacity — which is the box almost every European employer sits in the moment a member of staff opens ChatGPT

candidates.name: Vendor Cert Ladder
candidates.summary: A learner follows a structured, vendor-provided certification ladder from zero AI knowledge to a credentialled, verifiable proof of literacy. The external credential signals readiness to employers and creates a shared vocabulary within teams.
mechanics: Learner selects a foundational AI cert aligned to their ecosystem (Google, Microsoft, AWS, IBM, Anthropic, OpenAI).
Complete self-paced modules covering: what AI is, what it can do, responsible use, and at least one practical tool.
Pass a proctored or auto-graded assessment to earn a verifiable badge or certificate.
Employer maps cert tiers to internal role expectations (e.g. everyone = Foundational; builders = Associate).
Recertify on a defined schedule as ecosystems and model capabilities evolve.
proof: A verifiable digital badge or certificate linked to a learner identity.
Learner can apply the certified skills on a real task within their current role.

known_uses.name: Google AI Essentials
known_uses.quote: 67% of workers who used GenAI estimated using AI saves them 2+ hours per week
known_uses.note: 5 modules, <5 hours, no technical prerequisites, earns a Google Certificate. Available on Coursera for ~$49/month.

known_uses.name: Microsoft Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900 / AI-901)
known_uses.quote: Both certifications are designed for candidates from technical and non-technical backgrounds—prior experience in data science or software engineering is not required
known_uses.note: AI-900 retiring June 2026, replaced by AI-901. Available in 13 languages. ACE college credit eligible.

known_uses.name: AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)
known_uses.quote: The entire exam content outline for AWS Certified AI Practitioner focuses on AI, ML, and generative AI
known_uses.note: Launched October 2024. 90-minute exam, $100, 12 languages. Target: business analysts, marketers, project managers. 47% salary uplift cited for AI-skilled IT professionals.

known_uses.name: Anthropic AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations
known_uses.quote: This course has something for everyone, whether you're new to Claude or a seasoned AI practitioner.
known_uses.note: Free. Designed with Prof. Joseph Feller (UCC) and Prof. Rick Dakan (Ringling College). Teaches the 4D framework: Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence.

known_uses.name: IBM SkillsBuild AI Literacy
known_uses.quote: recognize, evaluate, and use AI responsibly
known_uses.note: Free, 4 hours, earns an IBM Digital Badge via Credly. Open to students, job seekers, educators, and lifelong learners.

known_uses.name: OpenAI AI Foundations (OpenAI Academy)
known_uses.quote: OpenAI is accelerating its push to certify 10 million Americans by 2030
known_uses.note: Delivered inside ChatGPT. Piloted 2025–2026 with Walmart, John Deere, BCG, Hearst. Uses ETS and Credly for psychometric rigour.

candidates.name: National-Scale Literacy Drive
candidates.summary: A government or national body funds and deploys a free, multi-language AI literacy programme targeting citizens or the whole workforce, not just tech professionals. Scale is the goal; the programme sets a shared national baseline and unlocks deeper workforce tracks.
mechanics: Government or national research body commissions course content from universities or expert consortia.
Course is made free, self-paced, and available in the national language(s); translated into regional or partner languages.
Distribution channels include national skills platforms, employers, and schools; completion is incentivised (SkillsFuture credits, certificates, employer recognition).
A measurable scale goal anchors the programme (e.g. 1% of EU citizens, 1 million prompters in Dubai).
Ongoing: courses are refreshed as AI capabilities change; new modules added for specific roles or sectors.
proof: Measurable enrolment milestone reached (e.g. millions enrolled, defined % of population).
Completion certificates recognised by employers for hiring or progression.

known_uses.name: Elements of AI (Finland / EU)
known_uses.quote: AI is going to have as big an impact on our society as electricity
known_uses.note: 2 million+ learners, 170+ countries, 40+ languages. Finland's EU Presidency 2019 gift: offer to all Member States for free with goal to train 1% of EU citizens.

known_uses.name: Singapore SkillsFuture AI programmes
known_uses.quote: 97% increase in employer demand for the 'AI Application' skill
known_uses.note: State-funded, subsidised up to 70%, 1784+ AI courses on the portal as of 2024. Workers receive SkillsFuture credits to spend on approved courses.

known_uses.name: One Million Prompters (Dubai / UAE)
known_uses.org: Dubai Future Foundation / Dubai Centre for AI
known_uses.quote: We want to be the most future-ready city and continue preparing for the AI era by developing expertise and skills that support global technological transformation
known_uses.note: Launched May 2024. 4 modules × 1 hour. Target: 1 million individuals trained in AI and prompt literacy over 3 years. Arabic-language delivery.

known_uses.name: Japan MEXT Mathematics, Data Science and AI Education Programme (Literacy Level)
known_uses.quote: all humanities and science students of universities and colleges of technology should master introductory-level mathematics, data science and AI
known_uses.note: 382 certified Literacy-Level programmes across Japanese universities as of Aug 2023. Co-administered by MEXT, Cabinet Office, and METI.

candidates.name: 4D Fluency Framework
candidates.summary: A structured four-part mental model — Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence — that gives learners a lasting cognitive scaffold for working with AI, not just instructions for a specific tool. The framework is designed to stay useful as models change.
mechanics: Teach each D in sequence: what tasks to delegate to AI (Delegation); how to write clear requests (Description); how to evaluate outputs critically (Discernment); how to use AI safely and ethically (Diligence).
Anchor each D to real worked examples from the learner's own domain.
Run a Description–Discernment loop exercise: write a prompt, evaluate the output, revise the prompt, repeat.
End each module with a Masterpiece task that requires all four Ds in sequence.
Provide the framework as a Creative Commons artefact so organisations can build home-forged courses on top of it.
proof: Learner can self-assess against each of the four Ds before and after the course.
Learner completes a Masterpiece task that requires all four Ds.

known_uses.name: Anthropic AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations
known_uses.quote: I want to prepare people for the long term. Not just how to use our latest features, but how to think about and work with AI—now and into the future.
known_uses.note: Developed with Prof. Joseph Feller (University College Cork) and Prof. Rick Dakan (Ringling College). Courses available for students, educators, and the general workforce. Free, with certificate of completion.

known_uses.name: Anthropic AI Fluency for Educators
known_uses.quote: empowers faculty, instructional designers, and educational leaders to apply AI Fluency into their own teaching practice and institutional strategy
known_uses.note: Demonstrates how the 4D framework is adapted for the educator persona — showing the persona-specific variant of this move.

sources.title: AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations
sources.quote: The framework consists of four pillars: Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence

sources.title: Anthropic's AI Fluency Course: How to Upskill Your Org with the 4D Framework
sources.quote: Margaret Vo, Head of Education at Anthropic, stated: 'I want to prepare people for the long term. Not just how to use our latest features, but how to think about and work with AI'

candidates.name: Responsible-Use Guardrail
candidates.summary: Every training track at every Step includes an explicit module on responsible use: what not to do, what to check, and what to report. This guardrail runs cross-cutting through the Craft Path so safety norms are instilled before bad habits form.
mechanics: Define the org's AI use policy before any training lands; the policy is the content of the guardrail module.
Cover at minimum: hallucination risk, data privacy, IP and copyright, misuse detection, and how to escalate.
Embed the guardrail at the start of every Step — not only at Foundation — so it is revisited as capability grows.
Use real incident examples (anonymised) to show what non-compliance looks like and costs.
Require acknowledgement (not just completion) — learner confirms they have read and understood the policy.
proof: Every learner has a timestamped, documented acknowledgement of the responsible-use policy.
Incident rate (accidental data leaks, copyright breaches) decreases after guardrail module is in place.

known_uses.name: MasterCard responsible AI principles training
known_uses.quote: Eight hours of video training on responsible AI principles (fairness, transparency)
known_uses.note: Delivered as dedicated company-wide track, not appended to a tool tutorial.

known_uses.name: AWS Certified AI Practitioner — Domain 4: Guidelines for Responsible AI
known_uses.quote: Domain 4: Guidelines for Responsible AI (14% of scored content)
known_uses.note: Responsible use is a graded domain in the foundational AWS AI cert, not an elective.

known_uses.name: Google AI Essentials — Module 4: Use AI Responsibly
known_uses.quote: 5 modules: Introduction to AI, Maximize Productivity With AI Tools, Discover the Art of Prompting, Use AI Responsibly, Stay Ahead of the AI Curve
known_uses.note: Responsible use is the fourth of five modules — not an afterthought.

known_uses.name: EU AI Act Article 4 — role-specific literacy including risk
known_uses.quote: taking into account their technical knowledge, experience, education and training and the context the AI systems are to be used in, and considering the persons or groups of persons on whom the AI systems are to be used
known_uses.note: Article 4 requires risk-context awareness, making responsible-use a legally-grounded component of operator literacy.

candidates.name: Reverse Mentoring for AI
candidates.summary: Junior, AI-fluent employees are paired as mentors to senior leaders, walking them through real AI workflows on the leaders' own tasks so executives gain hands-on fluency rather than briefings. It flips the usual direction of mentoring to move tacit AI know-how up the hierarchy.
mechanics: Identify AI-fluent juniors (often Gen Z / digital natives) and match them to senior leaders, sometimes in small circles
Frame it as reciprocal: leaders share strategy/business context, juniors share AI tooling
Work on the leader's real tasks (research, presentations, client comms) inside the actual tools, not slideware
Run on a light recurring cadence with psychological safety so leaders can admit what they don't know
Surface use-cases and concerns upward; feed them into adoption strategy
proof: IWG research: 72% of younger workers said their coaching improved overall team productivity, and 77% of directors agreed Gen Z's AI knowledge boosted their department's performance.
IWG: 62% of Gen Z employees are actively helping their senior colleagues upskill in AI.

known_uses.name: Reverse mentorship for AI upskilling (IWG-tracked trend)
known_uses.quote: 72% of younger workers said their coaching has improved overall team productivity, with 77% of directors agreeing that Gen Z's AI knowledge has boosted their department's performance

known_uses.name: CEO Global Reverse Mentor Program
known_uses.quote: ELC's CEO Global Reverse Mentor Program allows employees to share insights and ideas with senior leaders.
known_uses.note: Predates the AI focus but is cited as a model for AI reverse mentoring; source is the company's own social post.

sources.title: AI is accelerating the reverse mentorship trend
sources.quote: Sixty-two percent of Gen Z employees are actively helping their senior colleagues upskill in AI

candidates.name: Prompt Kata
candidates.summary: Short, self-contained, repeatable exercises that isolate one AI skill (prompting, reasoning, structured output, agent design) and can be attempted in minutes and revisited as you improve. Borrows the martial-arts/coding-kata idea of deliberate practice applied to working with AI — distinct from a multi-day dojo in that each kata is a single short rep.
mechanics: Pick one bite-sized kata that isolates a single ability (e.g. chain-of-thought, schema-constrained output)
Attempt it in a few minutes against a real model
Evaluate whether the technique actually improved the result
Revisit the same kata later as skills grow; progress to harder, multi-agent katas
Work through a graded catalog from fundamentals to complex systems
proof: The o3-cloud/ai-katas open catalog runs 100 katas progressing from simple fundamentals to multi-agent and multi-modal systems.

known_uses.name: AI Katas
known_uses.quote: A comprehensive collection of 100 AI katas progressing from simple fundamentals to complex, multi-agent and multi-modal systems.

sources.title: Introducing AI Katas: A Systematic Practice Framework for Modern AI Skills
sources.quote: Mastery now requires deliberate practice: structured, repeatable exercises that build durable skills in prompting, reasoning, evaluation, agent design, safety, and system architecture.

sources.title: o3-cloud/ai-katas
sources.quote: Each kata is designed as a hands-on task for practicing and mastering AI prompting and system design skills.

candidates.name: AI Café
candidates.summary: A short (1h-1h30), free, no-prerequisite convivial gathering where ordinary people come to discuss, question, and try out AI in a welcoming setting — France's national Café IA acculturation device run via libraries, médiathèques and digital-mediation networks. It is a foundation/acculturation Move: the goal is collective, democratic understanding of AI and a calmer public relationship with it, not technical skill-building.
mechanics: A facilitator (often a digital-mediation worker) hosts an informal 1h-1h30 session, free and open to anyone
No technical prerequisite; format mixes listening, debate, and hands-on tool manipulation
Participants bring their own questions and concerns about AI and discuss them collectively
Facilitators can use a ready-made toolkit (La Mallette IA) to structure accessible discussion
Sessions recur to build an ongoing public conversation
proof: A national device structured by the Conseil national du numérique since 21 May 2024.
Supported by La Mallette IA, a turnkey facilitator kit, and rolled out through ANCT digital-mediation networks and public libraries.

known_uses.name: Café IA (dispositif national)
known_uses.quote: Depuis le 21 mai 2024, le Conseil national du numérique est pleinement engagé dans la structuration de Café IA, un dispositif national de débats et de partage de connaissances sur l'intelligence artificielle.

known_uses.name: La Mallette IA (kit pour animer un Café IA)
known_uses.org: ANCT / Les Bases du numérique d'intérêt général
known_uses.quote: La Mallette IA a été conçue pour accompagner l'animation des Cafés IA et regroupe des outils pratiques et pédagogiques pour faciliter l'acculturation à l'intelligence artificielle.

sources.title: Participer à Café IA | Conseil national du numérique
sources.quote: un dispositif national de débats et de partage de connaissances sur l'intelligence artificielle

sources.title: Café IA | Les Bases du numérique d'intérêt général
sources.quote: Un Café IA est informel, frugal, gratuit et pour tout le monde.
